title: Scientist
description: Spark your curiosity and creativity with the Scientist activity badge. Have fun learning some valuable science-based skills.

tips.title: You can complete this badge individually, in Patrols, or in other small groups.
For option 1, you could investigate any activity completed as part of Scouts, such as archery, climbing or zip lining, or a hobby you do outside of Scouts, such as cooking, trampolining or swimming.
If you choose to do a presentation to share your knowledge with others, this could be in the form of a video or animation.

For option 2, you could share what you have discovered with your Patrol, Troop, another Group, a leader or someone else. There are lots of ways you could do this. Why not plan a demonstration, a presentation, run an activity or create a video?
Guidance for leaders
tips.details: Make sure that the experiments and activities that Scouts are doing are safe, particularly when it comes to making changes. Information about planning and assessing risk can be found at [scouts.org.uk/safety](http://www.scouts.org.uk/safety).

tips.title: Why not see if there is a local STEM Ambassador who can provide some support?
tips.details: Find out more about STEM Ambassadors on the [STEM Learning website](https://www.stem.org.uk/stem-ambassadors).

tips.title: Why not tie this badge in with British Science Week in March?
This badge can contribute towards a CREST Award.
tips.details: You can gain a Discovery or Bronze CREST Award by completing option 1 as a project of at least five or ten hours. [Find out more about CREST Awards.](https://members.scouts.org.uk/crestawards)

requirementsIntro: To earn your badge choose one of the following options, then complete all of the tasks under your chosen option.
